hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'Corpus' is addressed directly — it is vocative
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'corpus' would be 'corporis' — 'Corpus' here is vocative
hints.Accusative: While 'corpus' could be accusative neuter, here it is the addressee of 'salva'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'corporis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Christus' — 'Christi' has the -i genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Christus' would be 'Christo' — 'Christi' ends in -i (genitive)
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Christus' would be 'Christe' — 'Christi' is genitive

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 3rd sg. would be 'salvat' — 'salva' is the imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'salves' — 'salva' is the imperative singular of a 1st conjugation verb
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'salvare' — 'salva' is the imperative singular

hints.saluto, salutare, salutavi, salutatum: These are the principal parts of 'salutare' (to greet)
hints.sano, sanare, sanavi, sanatum: These are the principal parts of 'sanare' (to heal)
hints.servo, servare, servavi, servatum: These are the principal parts of 'servare' (to keep/preserve)
